back_translation: But the people of Israel acted unfaithfully regarding the things devoted to destruction. Achan son of Carmi, son of Zabdi, son of Zerah, of the tribe of Judah, took some of the devoted things. So the wrath of the LORD burned up against the people of Israel.
Meanwhile Joshua sent men from Jericho to the city of Ai, which is near Beth-aven east of Bethel, and told them, 'Go up and spy out the land.' So those men went up and spied out Ai.

They returned to Joshua and reported, 'There is no need for the whole army to go up. About two or three thousand men are enough to go up and strike Ai. Do not weary the whole army there, for the people of Ai are few.'
So about three thousand men went up there, but they fled before the men of Ai.
The men of Ai struck down about thirty-six of Israel's men, chased them from the gate as far as Shevarim, and attacked them on the slope. The hearts of the people melted and became like water.
Joshua tore his clothes and fell facedown to the ground before the ark of the LORD until evening — he and the elders of Israel — and they sprinkled dust on their heads.

Joshua said, 'Alas, O Sovereign LORD, why did You bring this people across the Jordan to deliver us into the hand of the Amorites to be destroyed? If only we had been content and stayed on the other side of the Jordan!
O, my Lord, what shall I say, now that Israel has turned its back and fled before its enemies?
When the Canaanites and all the inhabitants of the land hear this, they will surround us and cut off our name from the land. Then what will You do for Your great name?'
The LORD said to Joshua, 'Get up! Why have you fallen on your face?

Israel has sinned. They have also transgressed My covenant which I commanded them. They have also taken some of the devoted things. They have also stolen, also lied, and also put them with their own possessions.

This is why the people of Israel cannot stand before their enemies. They will turn their backs and flee before their enemies, because they themselves have become devoted to destruction. I will not be with you anymore unless you destroy the devoted things from among you.

Get up, consecrate the people, and tell them: "Consecrate yourselves for tomorrow, for this is what the LORD, the God of Israel, has said: O Israel, there are devoted things among you. You cannot stand before your enemies until you remove the devoted things from among you.

In the morning you must present yourselves tribe by tribe. The tribe which the LORD selects shall come forward by clans, and the clan which the LORD selects shall come forward by households, and the household which the LORD selects shall come forward man by man.

The one who is caught with the devoted things shall be burned with fire — he and all that belongs to him — because he has transgressed the covenant of the LORD and committed an outrageous thing in Israel."'
So Joshua got up early in the morning and had Israel come forward tribe by tribe, and the tribe of Judah was selected.
He had the clans of Judah come forward, and the clan of the Zerahites was selected. He had the clan of the Zerahites come forward man by man, and Zabdi was selected.
He had the household of Zabdi come forward man by man, and Achan son of Carmi, son of Zabdi, son of Zerah, of the tribe of Judah, was selected.
Joshua said to Achan, 'My son, give glory to the LORD, the God of Israel, and make confession to Him. Tell me what you have done; do not hide it from me.'
Achan answered Joshua, 'It is true — I have sinned against the LORD, the God of Israel. This is what I did:

When I saw among the spoil a beautiful cloak from Shinar, and two hundred shekels of silver, and a bar of gold weighing fifty shekels, I coveted them and took them. Look, they are hidden in the ground inside my tent, with the silver underneath.'
Joshua sent messengers, and they ran to the tent. Look! the things were hidden in his tent, with the silver underneath.
They took them out of the tent and brought them to Joshua and all the people of Israel, and laid them out before the LORD.

Then Joshua, together with all Israel, took Achan son of Zerah, the silver, the cloak, the bar of gold, his sons, his daughters, his ox, his donkey, his sheep, his tent, and everything that belonged to him, and brought them up to the Valley of Achor.
Joshua said, 'Why have you brought trouble upon us? Today the LORD will bring trouble upon you!' Then all Israel stoned him with stones, burned them with fire, and stoned them with stones.
They raised over him a great heap of stones that remains to this day. So the LORD turned from the burning of His anger. Therefore the name of that place is called the Valley of Achor to this day.
